title: SLSS 2025 Annual Report FA web
body:
ANNUAL REPORT & SEASON FINANCIAL STATEMENT 2024/25  PHOTOGRAPHY Glenn Duffus, Doug Hawkins, Premier’s Dept and our Clubs Surf Life Saving Sydney Branch PO Box 6006, Malabar NSW 2036 +61 (02) 9019 0722 sls.sydney admin@sls.sydney ABN 74 781 063 539 CFN 26951  01. Introduction 3 02. Branch Officers & Committees 13 03. Members 19 04. Awards of Excellence 33 05. Lifesaving & Rescue Services 45 06. Education 65 07. Youth & Junior Development 73 08. Surf Sports 79 09. Our Clubs 103 10. Financials 135 11. Administration 161

Contents

AN N UAL RE P O RT & FI N AN C I AL STAT E ME N T 2 0 2 4 / 2 5 1  2 S UR F LIFE SAV IN G SYDNEY  Introduction 01.

AN N UAL RE P O RT & FI N AN C I AL STAT E ME N T 2 0 2 4 / 2 5 3   It is with great pride and genuine admiration that I acknowledge the remarkable efforts of Surf Life Saving Sydney (SLSS) over the past year. As Patron, I am constantly reminded of the heart, courage and selflessness that define this incredible organisation. In the last twelve months alone, our dedicated lifesavers have patrolled our beaches for more than 188,000 hours. In that time, they performed 913 rescues, took more than 51,000 preventative actions, and safeguarded an estimated 1.7 million beachgoers. These figures speak volumes, but even more meaningful is what they represent. Lives protected, tragedies averted, and communities served with unwavering commitment. The increasing focus on proactive prevention has not only reduced the number of rescues needed but has also underscored the value of foresight, vigilance, and education on our shores. Youth engagement continues to be a shining priority. Through dynamic Camps and Clinics that combine learning with leadership, fun with challenge, SLSS is nurturing the next generation of lifesavers during those crucial years where retention

A MESSAGE can be a challenge. These young members are not just the future, they are already making a difference.It’s particularly inspiring

FROM OUR to see membership grow beyond 20,000 at a time when volunteering presents new challenges. The increasing representation of women, particularly in leadership roles is creating a more diverse, balanced, and forward-thinking movement. Patron

Congratulations to all members who were honoured nationally with the Order of Australia Medal and Emergency Services Medal. Your contributions uplift not only SLSS, but the broader community. I offer heartfelt congratulations to President Elissa Hancock and her dedicated Board of Directors. Your vision, energy, and devotion to innovation continue to guide this organisation with strength and purpose. Thank you for nurturing an environment where every member feels seen, supported and inspired. Finally, to each and every volunteer who gives their time, energy, and heart to this cause, thank you. Your actions save lives. Your example brings hope. And your presence on our beaches brings peace of mind to thousands. I wish you a safe, successful, and fulfilling summer season ahead. With deepest gratitude,

Peter Pearce OAM Patron

4 S UR F LIFE SAV IN G SYDNEY  AN N UAL RE P O RT & FI N AN C I AL STAT E ME N T 2 0 2 4 / 2 5 5  President

The 2024/25 Season brought out the very best in our Members. Across our beaches, clubs, and communities, we’ve witnessed exceptional service that has safeguarded countless lives, inspiring innovation that has strengthened our operations, and the unwavering dedication of our volunteers who give so freely of their time, skills, and energy. From the Patrolling Members on the sand to those working tirelessly behind the scenes, every individual has played a vital role in delivering the high standards of safety, education, and community connection that define Surf Life Saving Sydney. Time and time again, we’re reminded that Surf Life Saving is so much more than just an organisation. It’s a community, one built on care, shared purpose, and the belief that looking after others truly matters. Whether it’s on the beach, in the surf, in education courses or at youth events, the spirit of this movement continues to shine. Our reach has grown, but more importantly, our connections have deepened. This season, our Branch proudly surpassed 20,000 members, a powerful reflection of the trust our communities place in what we do. Together, our teams delivered more than 188,000 patrol hours, supported over 1.7 million beachgoers, completed 913 rescues, and carried out more than 51,000 preventative actions. Each number tells a real story of lives safeguarded, challenges met, and communities made stronger. We’re particularly proud of the shift we continue to see from reactive to proactive lifesaving. Prevention has become a cornerstone of our patrol efforts, and it’s making a real difference. Thanks to early interventions and member vigilance, fewer people have needed rescuing. It’s a powerful testament to the calm, capable presence of our lifesavers and the strength of our training programs. None of this would be possible without the incredible people behind it all. Our volunteers remain the very heart of this organisation. Whether you’re on the sand, mentoring a young lifesaver, helping behind the scenes, or leading from the front,

REPORT you matter. That’s why we are putting real effort into easing the burden on our clubs and improving support for members through better communication, leadership training, and access to mental health resources.

Our Vision Our Purpose Zero preventable deaths in Australian waters. We save lives, create great Australians and build Our Focus: Help our clubs and lifesavers be their better communities. Our Focus: Develop skilled best while striving to prevent drowning and injury lifesavers and leaders while fostering club and along the Sydney coastline. Surf Life Saving Sydney community collaboration and connection. (SLSS) Inc, is a coordination and representative Our purpose is to create a single uniform entity body of Surf Life Saving supporting our fifteen through and by which Surf Life Saving and the Surf Life Saving Clubs and various Rescue Services preservation of life in the aquatic environment can stretching from North Bondi south to Burning be conducted, encouraged, promoted, advanced Palms in the Royal National Park. and administered. We are a charitable community service-based Our principal activity for the 2024/25 season was organisations funded through donations, the provision of support to our members clubs fundraising, corporate sponsorship and which includes, club based patrols and support government grants. services, surf sports events, junior member growth and development along with support to our vibrant community of over 20,000 volunteers.

Our Values Our Priorities Our values are aspirational and guide our directors, officers PRIORITY 1 and members. We know what a good culture is - where the Making it easy for volunteers, staff and the community to behaviours match the rhetoric and we strive to incorporate contribute to the movement these values in our day to day operations. + Identifying pain points SAFETY – FIRST + Being a thought leader on change We develop a culture of safety with our members and with the public. + Be solution focused and leaders on what needs improving

TRUST + Advocating for it to be easier and better We develop trust through our actions. Protecting the flags and beaches on the frontline and by being open and honest with each other behind the line. PRIORITY 2 Growing and developing what we do best – saving lives RESPECT People come to the beach for fun. We respect the public, + Knowing our data the ocean and each other. We act with integrity and model + Develop growth priorities and strategies respectful behaviour. + Bring others along and guild alignment CARING + Provide greater service in better ways We support and exhibit concern for people on and off the beach. We value life and well being. + Connecting, collaborating and advocacy internally and externally RESPONSIBILITY We are compliant, accountable and deliver what we say we will.

8 S UR F LIFE SAV IN G SYDNEY  Donations and Sponsorship Surf Life Saving Sydney is proud to acknowledge our sponsor’s contribution, both financially and materially. Without sponsorship, ‘corporate, government and individual’, our task of providing viable lifesaving services to the community of Sydney would be impossible. We are proud that here in our Annual Report, the major public document of SLS Sydney, that we recognize and extend our sincere thanks to all our sponsors and donors for their support. Support that is given without restriction or interference with our objectives and achievements.

FUNDRAISING DISTRIBUTIONS GOVERNMENT RECOGNTION Surf Life Saving NSW and the Surf Life Saving Foundation should NSW Government for the support of: be acknowledged for conducting fundraising on behalf of clubs + The Emergency Services portfolio, for ongoing funding of and branches. Conducting fundraising efforts centrally frees state-wide operations and programs which benefit clubs, up our members’ time to focus their energy and resources on members and the community. lifesaving activities, their number one mission. It also helps to ensure greater equity of funding across NSW. + The Office of Sport, for their support to increase usage, safety and participation in Surf Life Saving through ongoing The generous support of the general public for the financial programs, specifically the Surf Cub facility grant program. distributions which enable SLS Sydney and its clubs and services to implement strategies to reduce downing and Federal Government for the support of: improve general public coast safety awareness. It assists with the purchase of rescue gear and equipment, training and + Beach Safety Equipment Funding, which directed $10,000 to assessment and public safety programs for members and the every Surf Club for the purchase of vital rescue equipment, public. first aid and medical supplies. + The Vocational Education Training Funding (VET) which enabled hundreds of assessors to update their training qualifications.

AN N UAL RE P O RT & FI N AN C I AL STAT E ME N T 2 0 2 4 / 2 5 9   Surf Life Saving Sydney Branch supports the Eastern and Southern Sydney coastline from Sydney Harbour down to the Royal National Park, with its 15 Surf Life Saving Clubs and Rescue Services. Over the 2024/25 Season, Sydney Branch had over 20k members, with, across our beaches, 188k patrolling hours, 1.7m estimated beachgoers, 913 individual rescues, and 51k preventions. Whilst these numbers are an impressive representation of the work that our members undertake, they demonstrate an increase of hours from last year of hours, with preventions increasing at a rate higher than the increase of our members. Thankfully more preventions means fewer rescues. The work that our volunteers are undertaking is continuing to increase year on year and our focus as a branch is to provide support to assist them in delivering these services. It is imperative that we do everything that we can to minimise the overheads and logistics within our clubs, and to upskill, develop President and promote our members in the delivery of their activities. Only by constantly reviewing the branch’s activities and events can we ensure that they remain relevant, timely, affordable and current, supporting our members as best they can. As a result, this season Sydney Branch has undertaken complete reviews across all our deliverables to identify what we deliver, and how it actually promotes the work that our amazing volunteers provide. From our outputs such as Youth Development, Member Services, Education, Lifesaving, Rescue Deputy

Services and Surf Sports, as well as our internal structures around IT, Finance and Strategy, we investigated and updated what, when, how and where we undertake our tasks to provide critical focus on our members and creating a better Sydney surf lifesaving community Our Portfolios have focused on identifying improvements such as, + In Rescue Services, we onboarded Surf Rescue 30 ensuring proper governance practices were applied. Updated governance frameworks across all Rescue Services reducing duplication, knocking down internal silos and creating greater pathways for our volunteers. We’ve now ensured our volunteers can seamlessly progress between services. + In Lifesaving we placed greater focus on the LSAs before implementation to reduce the burden on clubs during the review process. By ensuring clubs were informed they were able to engage with agency, delivering signed agreements far earlier than previously supplied + In Youth and Juniors we increased our Camps and Clinics, ensuring that our enthusiastic young members are provided with educational programs, social engagement, physical challenge, team building and development opportunities, enabling ongoing participation at a time when our young members are departing. + Our Members Services team redesigned their structure to ensure that focus aligned with membership needs. We hosted a Leadership & Development event for emerging talent, with guest speakers and networking opportunities, and a Mental Health First Aid Course with strong club representation keen for additional resources to support their vulnerable members

REPORT + Our Education team identified a backlog in processing our members and focused on identifying and onboarding new TAFs which enabled the backlog to be cleared.

Our clubs are now keen to be engaged, our Presidents actually look forward to our Branch Council I should also take the opportunity to thank Trent Thomas who Meetings, our members know what we actually do, our held this role until April 2025 and set the groundwork for volunteers are getting the support that they deserve, and the this transformation. Each of our office bearers is acutely aware of the challenges facing surf lifesaving. AN N UAL RE P O RT & FI N AN C I AL STAT E ME N T 2 0 2 4 / 2 5 21   Grahame Evans Life Member of Surf Life Saving Sydney Thursday 26 June 2025

It is with deep sadness that we share the passing of Grahame Evans. Grahame was a beloved Life Member of Surf Life Saving Sydney and a valued contributor to Cronulla SLSC. As we remember his remarkable life, we reflect on the lasting impact he made on our organisation and the wider surf lifesaving community. Serving as Sydney Branch Director of Surf Sports from 2003 to 2007 and honoured as a Life Member since 2011, Grahame embodied the very best of Surf Life Saving — his dedication, leadership, and unwavering support were a constant source of inspiration. His influence helped shape the identity and success of Surf Sports across our Branch, and his commitment Vale to excellence and passion for service will be remembered always. Grahame’s legacy is one of generosity, wisdom, and tireless devotion. The countless hours he gave, his infectious enthusiasm, and the strength of his character have left an indelible mark on us all. His contributions will continue to guide and inspire future generations. We extend our heartfelt condolences to Grahame’s family and friends. He will be deeply missed but never forgotten.
